title: Senior Quantity Surveyor
description:
Job role description:

  • The Senior Quantity Surveyor is responsible for taking and analysing measurements, as well as reviewing tender documentation. He/She will implement procurement strategies and review returned tender documents. He/She reviews cost estimates and cash flow forecasts to communicate updates to relevant stakeholders. He/She manages the complete post-contract administration processes. He/She leads project meetings with customers and supports with dispute resolution matters when required. He/She builds relationships with customers and liaises with contractors, specialists and sub-contractors to help develop resource plans and monitor costs and profitability of construction works to deliver the customers’ and/or project objectives.
  • He/She is analytical and detail-oriented. He/She is a team player with excellent leadership skills. He/She can guide his team on solving problems. He/She is organised and able to communicate effectively with various stakeholders. He/She will usually be stationed in the office or project work sites to perform his/her duties.

Key Tasks - Prepare measurements and tender documentation

· Analyse Building Information Modelling (BIM) models to validate cost plans

· Edit contract bills

· Analyse schedule of rates

· Draft preliminaries and trade preambles

· Review technical specifications prepared by technical consultants

· Analyse measurements, admeasurements, and re-measurements

Key Tasks - Drive cost planning and control

· Review cost analyses

· Review outline and detailed approximate cost estimates

· Analyse cost plans and detailed budgeting

· Review cost-in-use studies and life-cycle costing of building and installation and/or components

· Review cash flow projections

· Review cost checking processes during design development

· Interface with relevant stakeholders to advise alternative designs or methods of construction

· Review periodic cost reports

Key Tasks - Define tender and procurement strategies

· Carry out pre-qualifications

· Implement procurement strategies

· Review documentation to invite tenders from contractors, specialists or sub-contractors on a competitive or negotiated basis

· Evaluate tender returns

Key Tasks - Manage post-contract administration

· Advise on payment regimes

· Analyse claims on behalf of relevant parties

· Review valuations and recommendations for interim payments to contractors, specialists and sub-contractors

· Review and price assessment of variation cost claims

· Review final accounts

· Conduct contractual risk evaluations

· Lead project meetings

· Support the development of dispute resolution strategies


Job Requirements

  • Recognized Degree in Quantity Surveying, Building, or equivalent
  • Minimum 5 years of working experience in the Singapore building construction industry, with the Main contractor preferred
  • Good communicative skills, ability to interact with people of all levels and teamwork

postedCompany.description: Kajima Overseas Asia Singapore (KOAS) has strong Asian roots as we have been developing Singapore for more than 65 years. With more than 167 projects successfully completed, we have strong vision to continue being a responsible, reliable and committed construction partner for Singapore development for the next 65 years and more. In Kajima, we focus on creative progress and development based on rational, scientific analysis and humanitarian outlook to continue advancing our business operation and contribution to society. Aiming to develop a sustainable business in Singapore with a balance portfolio between public and private projects focusing on office, hospitality, healthcare, A&A works, mix-used development, industrial, semiconductor, data centre, commercial and retail.
postedCompany.name: KAJIMA OVERSEAS ASIA (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D16 Upper East Coast, Bedok, Eastwood, Kew Drive
